name: g15_transport_face_assignment
purpose: Assign tetra-native faces for the transport system in a way that preserves the observed 3-plus-1 closure reading.
core_claim: Faces are tetra-native; chirality is transported; the shell is a projection.

face_model.statement: The tetrahedron is read as three manifest faces coming together and one closure face folded in.

F0.name: left_manifest_face
F0.description: Left visible face in the front-face tetra reading.

F1.name: right_manifest_face
F1.description: Right visible face in the front-face tetra reading.

F2.name: base_manifest_face
F2.description: Lower/front visible face in the front-face tetra reading.

F3.name: closure_face
F3.description: The fourth face that folds in to complete tetrahedral closure.

emergent_reading.statement: The transport lens first presents an open trihedral shell. The tetrahedron is completed when F3 folds in.

face_adjacency.statement: Tetra adjacency is primitive: every tetra face meets the other three along fold edges.

witness_reading.interpretation: The observer stands outside the tetra and sees the manifest shell first. Closure is completed by folding in the fourth face.

oriented_face_states.statement: Face identity and transported orientation are distinct. The raw face label is tetra-native; the sign is carried by lifted transport.

chirality_assignment.statement: Chirality is not a primitive face label. Chirality emerges from the oriented folding of the closure face and from transport that retains sheet memory.
chirality_assignment.closure_rule: The same face may appear in opposite transported orientation as Fi+ or Fi-.

projection_to_shell.statement: Upstairs, downstairs, and stairs are not primitive tetra faces. They are shell projections of oriented face transport.

interpretive_rules: Do not treat cube faces as primitive ontology.
Do not assign chirality before sheet-aware transport is retained.
Do not flatten the closure face into a mere visual artifact.
Treat the manifest triad as presentation and the fourth face as completion.
Use tetra faces as primitive and shell regions as projected.

bridge_to_existing_specs.transport_emergence_stack: Places face emergence at projection and chirality emergence at transport.
bridge_to_existing_specs.transport_shell_grammar: Provides the shell reading used for projection.
bridge_to_existing_specs.transport_state_machine: Provides the sheet-aware lifted state needed for oriented face states.
bridge_to_existing_specs.transport_host_state_machine: Orders oriented face states through host chronology.

short_forms: three manifest faces plus one closure face
oriented faces live at the lift layer
shell regions are projected, not primitive
